approach: Interaction-Intent Boundary First: treat the next iteration as a semantic cleanup of mixer refresh intent before adding more UI surface, drawing a hard line between automatic state reconciliation and explicit user recovery actions.
selected_approach: Synthesized strategy: Intent-First Mixer Refresh Contract. Guide the next planner around one explicit semantic boundary: automatic mixer refreshes are reconciliation and must dedupe or stay quiet after failures, while explicit user actions are recovery intent and must be able to retry. Preserve scene-level freshness unless inspection proves same-scene request identity is needed, but document and fence the current reducer contract so legacy fields and future callbacks cannot bypass it casually.
